title: if, else if, else Statements
description: Learn to make decisions in your code using conditional statements.

slides.title: Making Decisions 🤔
slides.content:
# if Statements

In programming, we often need to execute code only when a certain condition is met. R provides `if`, `else if`, and `else` for this purpose.

Basic syntax:

```r
if (condition) {
  # code to run if condition is TRUE
}
```

The condition must evaluate to a single `TRUE` or `FALSE` (not a vector).

slides.title: Simple if Example

quizData.question: What happens if the condition in an if statement is FALSE?
quizData.explanation: If FALSE, the code block is skipped.

slides.title: if...else
Multiple Conditions with else if
else if Example

quizData.explanation: 75 is not >80, but it is >60, so the second branch executes, printing 'Medium'.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Conditions must evaluate to a single logical value. If you have a logical vector, wrap it in `any()` or `all()` to reduce to a single TRUE/FALSE.
